title: Spin Framework
subtitle: The developer CLI behind Akamai Functions

steps.label: The Analogy
steps.body: Spin is the IKEA assembly kit for Wasm functions. One CLI, every language, flat-pack instructions — no proprietary tooling, no platform lock-in.

steps.label: How It Works
steps.body:
Spin is the open-source developer framework and CLI for building Wasm applications. CNCF sandbox project. The developer workflow:

- `spin new` — scaffold a project from a template (Rust, Go, JS, Python)
- `spin build` — compile to Wasm
- `spin up` — run locally with full HTTP server
- `spin aka deploy` — push to Akamai Functions globally

Same artifact runs locally, on SpinKube (Kubernetes), and on Akamai Functions. Portability is the headline.

steps.label: Real World
steps.body: A Spin app is just a Wasm binary plus a `spin.toml` manifest declaring HTTP triggers, KV bindings, and outbound network grants. The capability-based security model: by default a function can't see filesystem, network, or env vars unless explicitly granted.
"Spin = the open-source framework and CLI. Same Wasm binary runs local → Kubernetes → Akamai Functions. Zero lock-in."

quiz.question: How does a Spin function get permission to make HTTP calls or read a key from KV?

options.text: It can do anything by default
options.explain: Opposite — Wasm modules are locked-in by default. Capabilities must be explicitly granted.

options.text: Capabilities are explicitly declared in spin.toml
options.explain: Capability-based security. Outbound network, KV stores, and other resources are listed per-component in the manifest.
Env vars don't grant network or storage access.

options.text: You compile with a special flag
options.explain: It's a runtime/manifest concern, not a compile flag.

quiz.question: Same Spin app should run locally for development, on Kubernetes for staging, on Akamai Functions for production. What changes between them?

options.text: You rewrite the app for each platform
options.explain: No rewrite — that's the point of Spin's portability.

options.text: The Wasm binary is identical; only the deployment target changes
options.explain: Same Wasm artifact, same spin.toml. Local = `spin up`, K8s = SpinKube, Akamai = `spin aka deploy`.
Multi-language: Rust, Go, JS, Python, anything that compiles to Wasm.

options.text: You need a different framework per environment

free.title: SpinKube for Kubernetes
free.meta: Free · run Spin in your cluster
